text.title: Cheap-Guardrail Tripwire Core Rules
text.summary: Four testable rules every input-guardrail enforces.

rule.statement: The guardrail uses a model at least one tier cheaper than the main agent (e.g. haiku for sonnet, mini for sonnet, flash-lite for sonnet/opus). Same-tier guardrail negates the savings.

rationale.source: OpenAI Agents SDK guardrail design; production cost analysis

rule.rationale: The whole value is cost asymmetry: noise filtered at ~1% of main-model price.

rule.statement: Verdict is a strict-mode structured output: `{tripwire_triggered: bool, reason: enum, confidence: float}`. No free-text.
rule.rationale: Free-text verdicts can't be reliably gated; typed schema is the contract.

rule.statement: Guardrail is a SINGLE cheap LLM call. No tool use, no chain. Anything more is just another agent.
rule.rationale: Multi-call guardrail loses the cost asymmetry and the latency budget.

rule.statement: False-positive rate is monitored against a labelled calibration set. FP rate > 2% triggers prompt or threshold revision.
rule.rationale: Unmonitored guardrail drifts toward over-refusal; legitimate users get blocked.
